content: p Charter Communications, operator of the Spectrum cable brand, has obtained Federal Communications Commission permission to buy Cox and surpass Comcast as the country's largest home Internet service provider.  p   p Charter has  a href    million  a  residential and business Internet customers compared to  a href    31.26 million  a . Buying Cox will give Charter another  a href    million Internet customers  a . The FCC  a href    the deal on Friday, but the companies still need Justice Department approval and sign-offs from states including California and New York.  p   p Opponents of Charter's  a href    billion  a  acquisition told the FCC that eliminating Cox as an independent entity will make it easier for Charter and Comcast to raise prices. But the FCC dismissed those concerns on the grounds that Charter and Cox don't compete directly against each other in the vast majority of their territories.  p  p  a href    full article  a   p   p  a href

metadata.author: Jon Brodkin
